titleStmt.title: Letter from Eithne A. Kelly to Nancy O'Rahilly, 14 May 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Eithne A. Kelly

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter of condolence from Eithne A. Kelly to Nancy O'Rahilly (1875-1961). The letter is an expression of sympathy for Mrs. O'Rahilly on the death of her husband, fighting in the Easter Rising. The writer says she prays for all those in the same situation.Nancy O'Rahilly was a member of the Provisional Council of Cumann na mBan at the founding in 1914 and the wife of Michael Joseph O'Rahilly, self-described as 'The' O'Rahilly (1875-1916). The O'Rahilly fought in the Easter Rising and was shot in Moore (Parnell) Street on 28 April 1916 and died some time later. Nancy was pregnant at the time of her husband's death – the child was born three months later.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
61, Anglesea Road Donnybrook. May 14. 16 My dear Mrs O'Rahilly- I should have
written before but that
I was afraid of dis
tressing you more .
God will give you
abundant reward for
your great sorrow &
your love & fealty to him. I pray for you &
all afflicted so heavily, every
time I kneel down â I wish I could do
anything for you. I
hope I may get the
opportunity some time, Yrs. affy Eithne A Kelly
